title: Exit Optionality

content.statement: Build the company to be sellable even if you never plan to sell.
content.question: What would make this business valuable without you personally holding it together?
content.equation: Business Value = Profit × Transferability × Founder Independence
content.mental_model: House with clean wiring, valuable whether you live in it or sell it.
content.business_lens: Document systems, reduce founder dependency, strengthen recurring revenue, and make the company understandable to buyers.
content.life_lens: Build your work so it gives you choices instead of trapping you inside your own creation.
sop: Ask what would make the company attractive to a buyer
content.explainer: Exit Optionality means you are not building only for income. You are building an asset. Even if you never sell, the discipline of building a sellable company creates cleaner systems, stronger leadership, and more freedom.
questions: What would make this business valuable without you personally holding it together?
If you disappeared for 90 days, would the business grow, stall, or collapse?
What's the one piece of institutional knowledge that only exists in your head right now?

definitions.def: The value of having future choices.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Profit 7, Transferability 4, Founder Independence 3. Score = 7 times 4 times 3 = 84 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means a buyer could step in and the business would keep running. Lower means the business is really just a job wearing a company's clothes.
content.closing_statement: Build an asset, not just an income.

caption:
Principle Nº 252: Exit Optionality

Build the company to be sellable even if you never plan to sell.

Ask yourself: What would make this business valuable without you personally holding it together?
